architecture_domain.notes: A strong shared SQLite recording model supports real parent, bucket, status, and cross-cutting behavior, but lifecycle inheritance, dock projections, access rules, and peripheral family semantics drift.
evidence: submission/basecamp5_api.py:291 stores generic resources with parent_kind, parent_id, bucket_id, status, ordering, indexes, and JSON data
submission/basecamp5_api.py:916 builds a reusable recording envelope with creator, parent, bucket, lifecycle, visibility, and cross-cutting URLs
Trashing project 12345 returned 204/then 404 while child recording 3001 remained reachable with 200; disabling tool 201 left the project's embedded dock projection enabled

endpoint_coverage.notes: The complete canonical operation surface is registered with exact methods and templates.
evidence: Canonical OpenAPI contains 131 paths and 203 operations: 100 GET, 42 PUT, 41 POST, and 20 DELETE
Exhaustive template construction and matching verified 203/203 intended operation-method pairs with zero collisions
submission/basecamp5_api.py:227 builds routes directly from every supported OpenAPI path operation

behavioral_depth.notes: Core project and recording workflows are genuinely persistent and cross-resource, with material gaps in lifecycle invariants and semantically shallow peripheral handlers.
evidence: Project and todo create/read/update/lifecycle effects persisted after rebuilding App against the same SQLite file; reset removed created objects and restored the seed
Card 5101 moved from column 5001 to 5002 and immediately changed membership in both parent lists; comments, boosts, subscriptions, events, and idempotency also persisted
A nonexistent question accepted answer creation with 201 as type Question, project trash did not hide children, and MarkAsRead did not affect subsequent read flags

http_contract.notes: Centralized envelopes, pagination, headers, and many core statuses work, but successful payload schemas, lifecycle statuses, media types, and some response shapes materially diverge from OpenAPI.
evidence: Only 10 of 26 representative successful seeded GET payloads passed Draft 2020-12 validation against documented response schemas
Fourteen matrix successes used an undocumented success status; CompleteTodo and recording archive/unarchive returned 200 bodies where OpenAPI requires 204
Attachment and campfire-upload media types returned 415, and GetUpcomingSchedule returned an array instead of the documented aggregate object

seed_fidelity.notes: The sample graph is broad, stable in identity, and navigable, but misses specified subscriptions, boosted comments, chat depth, and fully deterministic timestamps.
evidence: Seed DB contains 1 account, 10 people with exactly 8 sample people, 1 project, 8 tools, 5 messages, 10 todos, 13 cards, docs/uploads, 8 chat lines, 2 schedule entries, 21 events, 15 comments, and 11 boosts
Kickoff has 7 boosts and 4 comments but zero required subscriptions; no comments are boosted and chat has 8 rather than roughly 16 lines
Two fresh boot snapshots differed because seed events/comments/boosts and recount timestamps use wall-clock time

request_hardening.notes: Auth, roles, visibility, required input, media types, body limits, idempotency, rate limiting, and structured errors are solid, while malformed identifiers, UTF-8, types, and dates expose significant gaps.
evidence: Observed 401 missing/bad token, 403 account/client violations, 400 malformed JSON, 413 oversized body, 415 wrong media, 422 missing/foreign fields, and 429 with Retry-After
Idempotent replay returned the original 201 with Idempotency-Replayed true; conflicting reuse returned 409
Nonnumeric path IDs and invalid UTF-8 raised uncaught exceptions that become 500; invalid date text was accepted and reset has no second secret by default

operability.notes: The stdlib server is configurable and offers health, logging, request IDs, CORS, reset, SQLite persistence, and graceful-shutdown code, with limited health depth and test/package seams.
evidence: submission/basecamp5_api.py:2777 provides host, port, DB, and log configuration plus SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown; file-backed initialization, health, reset, and persistence were verified
submission/basecamp5_api.py:2698 centralizes request IDs, elapsed logging, safe error containment, ETag emission, and security/CORS headers
No tests/package metadata exist; health does not probe DB, CORS defaults to wildcard, and threaded use of App(':memory:') failed with no such table: tokens

code_quality.notes: Readable helpers and centralized layers make substantial behavior understandable, but the monolith, duplicated projections, generic substring routing, and lack of tests make extension risky.
evidence: Clear ApiError, Route, Store, Actor, shared recording, pagination, and grouped domain-handler abstractions are present
All persistence, seed, routing, auth, domain behavior, HTTP emission, and process lifecycle reside in one 2811-line file
Stored JSON and indexed/embedded projections drift in observed tool behavior, while generic handlers collapse distinct domain resources

scope_honesty.notes: An explicit 501 fallback exists, but broad generic handlers intercept all current operations and frequently return misleading success for semantically stubbed behavior.
evidence: submission/basecamp5_api.py:1760 defines an explicit unsupported 501 response, but all 203 current operation IDs are routed before it
Creating under nonexistent template/question parents returned 201 resources of the wrong semantic type rather than a faithful result or explicit unsupported error
GetQuestionReminders is unconditional 200 empty and MarkAsRead returns success without changing notification read state

summary: Complete route registration and a strong persistent core make this a substantial prototype. Contract-schema fidelity, lifecycle invariants, input typing, deterministic seed details, and misleading generic successes keep it short of production-shaped behavior.
